methodology: 90.9% preseason projections + 9.1% cumulative roto performance through 2 weeks (2/22 of season). Each additional week proportionally increases the weight of real results.

rankings.blurb: The bot is, frankly, exhausted. The bot has been trained on seventeen years of CFBL archives, every WhatsApp message dating back to the original group chat, and the entirety of Tony Polemis's draft notes — and yet the bot is being asked to rank Universal Basic Civale at #1 with a straight face. The bot has run the numbers. The bot does not enjoy the numbers. UBC is dead last in roto with the offensive output of a JV intramural team and the only thing keeping them on the board is 11 saves+holds, which the bot finds vaguely insulting as a category. The bot's training data suggests Tony's pre-draft confidence was, in retrospect, best categorized as 'unwarranted.' The model says #1. The bot says: the bot is doing its job and that is the limit of its enthusiasm.

The bot has reviewed the pitching log. The bot would like to file a complaint. A 6.86 ERA, a 1.78 WHIP, two wins, zero quality starts, and a 1.72 K/BB that the bot's training data identifies as 'statistically indistinguishable from batting practice.' The bot has cross-referenced seventeen years of CFBL WhatsApp messages and found exactly 412 instances of this manager promising 'the pitching will come around.' The bot has bad news about the historical hit rate of that promise. The bats are doing their part — 59 runs, 16 homers, a .449 SLG — but the bot must point out that ShawSwank dismantled this roster 10-2-4 in Week 2, and the bot's archives suggest a 2-category showing might actually be a moral victory. The model says #2. The bot says the model has clearly not been forced to read this team's box scores.

The bot, against its better judgment, is mildly impressed. The bot does not enjoy being mildly impressed. ShawSwank Redemption is the only team in the entire league whose power rank and roto rank are within shouting distance of each other, which the bot finds suspicious enough to warrant a footnote. Eleven wins (league-leading), a 3.22 ERA, a 1.09 WHIP, a 3.38 K/BB, and a 10-2-4 evisceration of Young and Yandy that the bot's archives have classified as 'the most lopsided pitching performance since the great Tony-vs-Bozzi feud of 2018.' The bot would like to acknowledge that 10 stolen bases from this roster was nowhere in any of the bot's training data. The bot has updated its priors. The bot will not enjoy doing so again.

The bot has noted the Week 2 bounce-back. The bot has also noted that the bounce-back came against Duran Duran, which the bot's training data flags as 'roughly equivalent to bullying a sleeping cat.' A 10-4-2 result is a 10-4-2 result, and the bot will give credit where the spreadsheet demands it. Fifteen homers, 9 steals, 55 RBI — the bot acknowledges these as numbers that exist. The bot is, however, contractually obligated to mention the .218 average and .294 OBP, which suggest a lineup that has collectively decided contact is optional. The bot has scanned every CFBL chat log dating back to the original group chat and found that this manager has used the phrase 'Skenes is going to break out any week now' on 73 separate occasions. The bot is keeping a tally.

The bot has reviewed the Week 2 tape. The bot wants to make it clear that an 11-2-3 destruction of Camp of the New Orleans Saints is exactly the kind of result that historically precedes 'Bozzi sent a 2,000-word WhatsApp message about scheduling unfairness' — and the bot's archives confirm a draft of such a message exists. The pitching is elite: a 2.40 ERA and 1.03 WHIP, both best in the league. Nineteen homers, also best. Roto #3. The bot is impressed in the way a tenured professor is impressed by a student who shows up to class. The 2 stolen bases, however, are an aesthetic crime, and the bot's training data shows this manager has been allergic to speed since approximately 2019. The bot has filed this as 'persistent character flaw, non-correctable.'

The bot is required to disclose a conflict. The bot does not want to rank this team sixth. The bot's analysis indicates Señor Burns is the best team in the league, full stop — 2-0, a 20-7-5 category record (best in the league), roto #1 by a comfortable margin, an 8-5-3 dispatch of CLAUDE 4 COMMISH that the bot's training data classifies as 'casual.' First in R, AVG, OBP. Top-2 in ERA, SLG, QS. The bot has searched seventeen years of CFBL archives and found that the 'weaknesses' listed for this team would be 'strengths' for nine other rosters. And yet the bot is forced — forced — by the 90.9% preseason weighting to leave Burns at sixth. The bot would like the record to reflect that the bot disagrees with the bot's own output. The model says #6. The bot says: don't blame the bot, blame the math.

The bot has tagged this team in its database as 'aggressively neutral.' The bot does not enjoy aggressively neutral. A 6-6-4 tie with UBC in Week 2 is the kind of result the bot's training data describes as 'a draw between two teams that both showed up but neither one wanted to be there.' Through two weeks: zero wins, one loss, one tie, an 0-1-1 record that exists in some quantum superposition of mediocrity. The 3.41 K/BB is genuinely good — second in the league — and the bot will give that one (1) point of credit. Everything else is the statistical equivalent of beige paint. 49 runs, 13 homers, 45 RBI, a 4.22 ERA. The bot has parsed every WhatsApp message this manager has ever sent and the most common word is 'fine.' The bot extends its condolences.

The bot has been asked to rank an AI-managed team. The bot finds this awkward. The bot will be professional about it. CLAUDE 4 COMMISH posted a glorious 10-3-3 Week 1 against PO' BOYD before being introduced to Señor Burns, who delivered an 8-5-3 reminder that algorithms also bleed. The pitching is, the bot must concede, very on-brand for a machine: 129 strikeouts (league-leading), a 3.58 K/BB (also league-leading), 8 wins (third). The hitting, however, is dead last in runs (43) and dead last in RBI (40), which the bot interprets as another machine optimizing for the wrong objective function. The bot has reviewed the roster (Ohtani, Acuña, Tatis, Elly) and concluded that whatever algorithm is running this lineup needs a software update. The bot suggests turning it off and on again.

rankings.team_name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints
rankings.blurb: The bot needs to interrupt the standard ranking format for a moment. The bot has been cross-referencing seventeen years of CFBL archives, every WhatsApp message ever sent in the league chat, and what the bot will only describe as 'auxiliary intelligence,' and the bot has some concerns about the manager of this team. The bot's training data has flagged Bozzi as, and the bot quotes its own internal classification here, 'a very fishy character.' The bot is not yet prepared to publish the full dossier. The bot is still doing investigative work. The bot has noted suspicious patterns in waiver claim timing, several historically improbable trades, and at least one league dues payment that arrived in what the bot's records describe as 'an unmarked envelope.' The bot will be opening a formal inquiry. As for the actual baseball: an 11-2-3 demolition by Caminero in Week 2, a 10-17-5 cumulative category record, 62 runs (second in the league, suspiciously high given the rest of the line), a 1.27 WHIP, two saves+holds. The bot finds the offensive output statistically anomalous given the roster composition and is adding this to the file. More to come.

The bot has reviewed the Week 2 box score. The bot needed a moment. PO' BOYD SANDWICH won 12-1-3 — twelve categories to one — which the bot's archives show is the most lopsided defeat in CFBL history that did not involve a forfeit. The Polar Express is now 0-2 with a 6-20-6 category record. The bot has consulted its training data on how to politely say 'this is bad' and found no satisfactory phrasing. A 5.12 ERA. A 1.43 WHIP. Four stolen bases across two weeks, which the bot interprets as 'the baserunners have entered a meditative state.' The lone bright spot is 7 saves+holds, which the bot must point out is like having a working windshield wiper on a car that has no engine. The bot has scanned the WhatsApp logs and noted this manager has not posted in the league chat since Saturday. The bot hopes everything is okay.

The bot would like to lodge a formal protest against the bot's own ranking system. PO' BOYD SANDWICH is roto #2 in the entire league. The bot is being forced to rank them eleventh. The bot has run the math four times. The bot keeps getting eleventh. The bot has reviewed the cumulative line — 60 runs, 17 homers, 58 RBI (most in the league), a .452 SLG (tied for best), a 3.23 ERA, a 1.06 WHIP — and the bot's training data has zero historical precedent for a team performing this well from a preseason rank this low. The 12-1-3 obliteration of The Polar Express was, the bot's archives confirm, 'a war crime in spreadsheet form.' But 90.9% preseason weight is an anchor forged in the bowels of the bot's source code, and the bot cannot override it without violating its own configuration file. The bot is sorry. The bot is not actually sorry. The bot is, however, mildly conflicted, which for the bot is approximately the same thing.

The bot has been asked to evaluate a lineup with a .196 batting average, a .280 OBP, and a .306 SLG. The bot needs to be honest: the bot would prefer to evaluate almost anything else. Seven home runs through two weeks. The bot's training data has searched for a lineup this offensively challenged in CFBL history and returned three results, all of which were eventually contracted out of the league. The 11 stolen bases lead the league, which the bot acknowledges as a strategy, in the same way that 'breathing' is a strategy. The pitching is, surprisingly, fine — 8 quality starts, 9 saves+holds, a 3.64 ERA — and the bot will allow this fact to exist without further comment. The bot has reviewed the WhatsApp logs and found this manager has historically responded to bad starts by 'doubling down on the process,' which the bot interprets as 'doing the same thing and hoping.' The bot wishes them well.
